name: claude-orchestrator
description: CTO-assistant template — macro architectural truth across one company's stack. Distributable kit modeled on claude-kit.

kit.description: Files synced into target instances. Updates here propagate via /sync.

files.note: CTO operating discipline. Generic across all instances. The orchestrator's analog of claude-kit's task-rules.md.
How the orchestrator works with sub-projects (kit-enabled vs not, read/write discipline, git management). Generic across all instances.
Pinned reference for claude-kit's file shape and conventions. Re-synced manually when claude-kit changes.
Design standards discipline doc — what's tracked, source-of-truth rules, validation pattern. Generic across instances.
Registration discipline + the /register and bin/setup flows. Workspace-internal model (sub-repos clone into repos/<name>/), mobile fallback, edge cases, anti-patterns. Generic across instances.
Per-sub-repo state file template. Each registered sub-repo gets state/sub-repos/<name>.md instantiated from this.
Collaborator-onboarding script — clones registered sub-repos into repos/<name>/. Synced from kit so improvements propagate; instances get the latest version on /sync.
Refresh script — fetches origin for every cloned sub-repo, writes state/last-fetch.json with ahead/behind/dirty per repo. Driven by /refresh skill; user-runnable directly. Synced from kit.

Walks markdown under kit/, bootstrap/, decisions/, features/, migrations/, proposals/, etc. and verifies every internal [text](path) link resolves. Catches stale-reference bugs at commit time. Run via pre-commit hook or CI. In kit-checkout mode it produces known false positives for instance-content references (top-level *.md from bootstrap, .claude/* synced files); accurate in instances.

Validates a task or proposal markdown file against governance/task-spec-shape.md: required frontmatter delimiters, all 9 required body sections, AC has checkboxes, References has bullets. Used by /promote (refuses to promote invalid spec) and /propose new (warns at draft time). Exit 0 valid, 1 invalid, 2 usage error.

Renders <sub>/.claude/active-<concern>.md content from the kit template + filtered source artifacts. Concerns: features (filters features/*.md by affects+status), migrations (filters migrations/active/*.md by affects). Wholesale regeneration; outputs nothing if no open items affect the repo (caller deletes the file). Used by /feature and /migration.

Standardized chore/orch-* PR flow for sub-repos: pull main, branch, expects caller to have written changes, commits with orch: prefix, pushes, opens gh PR. Used by /register (init-shared), /migration (notice updates), /promote (task drops), and any future skill that opens orchestrator-authored PRs.

Implements the 5-step recipe at a known fork from preferences.md. Subcommands: resolve (look up active preference), log (append decision-log entry, recompute aggregate, return offer signal), capture (write preference, archive log), revoke (move to revoked), clear-log (reset fork tally). Preferences-aware skills shell out to this instead of describing the algorithm in markdown.

Read/write helper for the central per-repo configuration at state/repos.json. Subcommands: list, exists, get, add, set, remove. Source of truth for registered sub-repos — every script that operates on a sub-repo reads from this. Replaces ad-hoc parsing of state/manifest.md.

Work-doing register script — verifies remote URL, detects default branch, queries gh API for kit-enabled status, writes state/repos.json + state/manifest.md + state/sub-repos/<name>.md, optionally clones into repos/<name>/ and scaffolds .claude/shared/. The /register skill is a thin wrapper that gathers args (interactive Q&A) and presents the script's output.

Discipline doc — when to script vs interpret skills. Decision rule, config-as-args pattern, generic-vs-templated scripts, file-layout conventions, output-pattern selection requirement. Generic across instances.
Phase/task discipline contracts the orchestrator and sub-kits both follow: phases-and-tasks.md, task-spec-shape.md, dev-preflight.md. Generic across instances.
Operating preferences discipline — how the orchestrator captures and applies recurring user decisions to reduce friction. The dual to orchestrator-rules.md 'When in doubt'. Generic across instances.

Visual design catalogue — 34 terminal output patterns. Every orchestrator script picks a pattern from here for its output shape. Consistency across scripts means a reader who's learned one knows how to read all the others. Generic across instances.
Each subdirectory under kit/skills/ is a skill. Mirrored into the instance's .claude/skills/.
ADR template and format docs.
Feature plan template and format docs.
Migration template and format docs. Top-level files only — does not touch active/ or closed/.
Proposal staging area templates (_template-task.md, _template-PHASES.md, README.md). Top-level files only — does not touch per-repo subdirectories created lazily by /propose.
Risk register template and format docs. Top-level files only — does not touch open/ or mitigated/ instance content.
Incident template and format docs. Top-level files only — does not touch instance incident files.
Review templates (weekly/monthly/quarterly) + format docs. Top-level files only — does not touch weekly/ monthly/ quarterly/ instance content.
PR review template + format docs (orchestrator-grade macro reviews). Top-level files only — does not touch instance review files.
State directory format documentation.
Template files used by skills (e.g. /migration reads sub-repo-notices/migrations.md.template). Lives under .claude/ because it's kit machinery, not user-authored content.

bootstrap.description: One-time files copied at init only. Never overwritten by /sync.

files.note: Stamped with the kit's HEAD commit SHA at init time.
